concept: Shallow Copy vs Deep Copy (The Reference Trap)

executionTimeline.description: The 'original' object is created on the heap. The variable 'original' holds a reference (address) to this memory.
The spread operator creates a new object. It copies 'name' (primitive) by value, but it copies 'details' (object) by REFERENCE.
JSON stringify/parse creates a completely new set of objects on the heap for all levels.
Changing 'name' on shallowCopy only affects Ref@303. Ref@101 (original) remains 'Alice'.
Changing 'details.age' on shallowCopy modifies Ref@202. Since original also points to Ref@202, BOTH are changed!
Printing the results. The name is preserved, but the age is mutated.

theFix: To avoid mutating the original state, use structuredClone(original) for a true deep copy, ensuring that nested objects are also duplicated in memory rather than shared.
